title: [Tyranitar] Battle Guide
summary: A [Rock]/[Dark] [tank] attacker that auto-sets [sand-stream] and hits the whole meta with [stone-edge], [crunch], and [earthquake].
strengths: [sand-stream] fires the moment it switches in and buffs [Rock]-type Special Defense by 50%
134 Attack plus [stone-edge] / [crunch] / [earthquake] covers almost every defensive core
100 HP and 110 Defense makes it a genuine [tank]-attacker, not just a wallbreaker
weaknesses: 4x [Fighting] — [close-combat] from Keldeo or Conkeldurr ends it instantly
Also 2x to [Ground], [Bug], [Grass], [Water], [Steel], and [Fairy] — seven weaknesses total
Base 61 Speed is outrun by nearly every unboosted meta attacker

recommendedMoves.reason: Reliable [Dark] STAB to handle [Ghost] and [Psychic] threats
Removes the base 61 Speed problem in a [sing]le turn

recommendedItems.reason: No sand chip for [Rock] types plus 6% passive heal every turn

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Tyranitar] is a [Rock]/[Dark] [tank]-attacker whose entire kit is built a[round] [sand-stream]. The ability auto-sets sand the moment it enters, which buffs [Rock]-type Special Defense by 50%, turning 100/110/100 into effective defensive stats a[round] 100/110/150. Combined with 134 Attack and broad cove[rage], it plays as a bulky wallbreaker that wins long games, not a one-shot nuke.

## Strengths
The [sand-stream] SpD buff is the most important stat interaction on the mon — it means [thunderbolt] and [ice-beam] no longer 2HKO, opening safe setup turns that [Tyranitar] normally couldn't afford. [stone-edge], [crunch], and [earthquake] form a three-direction cove[rage] net with almost no shared resists across the meta. [Rock]/[Dark] STAB already hits [Ghost], [Psychic], [Flying], [Ice], and [Fire], while [earthquake] cleans up [Steel], [Fire], and [Electric] switch-ins trying to [tank] the STAB combo.

## Weaknesses & Answers
4x [Fighting] is the bill-killer — Keldeo's [secret-sword] and Conkeldurr's [drain-punch] both one-shot through any investment. 2x weaknesses to [Ground], [Bug], [Grass], [Water], [Steel], and [Fairy] stack seven total weaknesses, so switch-in decisions are constrained. Base 61 Speed loses to everything outside of [trick-room], and the sand chip hurts any non-[Rock]/[Ground]/[Steel] teammates, so whole-team synergy matters.

## Recommended Moveset
[stone-edge] is the main STAB — accuracy risk is accepted for the raw OHKO thresholds it reaches. [crunch] is the clean [Dark] STAB for [Ghost] and [Psychic]. [earthquake] is mandatory cove[rage] for [Steel], [Fire], and [Electric] threats that otherwise wall [Rock]/[Dark]. The last slot is [dragon-dance] — one turn of setup erases the Speed problem and converts [Tyranitar] into a [tank]-sweeper. Swap [dragon-dance] for stealth-[rock] on hazard-lead variants where [Tyranitar] is used purely as a weather installer.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the pick because [Rock] types don't take sand chip, so the 6% heal is pure profit. [sand-stream] is the locked ability and the whole reason to run [Tyranitar] on a sand team. Tera [Flying] is the default — it removes the 4x [Fighting] weakness entirely. Tera [Fighting] is the offensive alternative that powers up [close-combat] while trimming some weaknesses.

## Teammates & Synergy
Sand abusers (Excadrill, Bariquool lines) share the buff and justify building the team a[round] [Tyranitar]'s ability. Dedicated [Fighting] switch-ins (Latias, Metagross) are mandatory to cover the exact weakness that [revenge]s it. A separate hazard lead lets [Tyranitar] c[lick] [dragon-dance] instead of stealth-[rock]. [Ghost] partners deny [defog] and keep the hazards alive for the sweep.

## Play Patterns
Lead [Tyranitar] to set sand turn 1 and scout the oppo[sing] lead. If the matchup is favorable, c[lick] [dragon-dance] immediately; otherwise cycle out and come back in the mid-game as a [tank]-attacker. C[lick] [stone-edge] only when accuracy risk is acceptable — otherwise default to [crunch] and [earthquake] for reliable damage. Tera the moment a [close-combat] is telegraphed to erase the 4x weakness.
